_meta.description: Commander archetype synergy database. Each archetype maps keyword inputs to expanded oracle-text search patterns, required deck roles, and a strategy directive injected into the LLM prompt.

sacrifice.description: Generate repeatable value from creatures dying. Needs sac outlets, death payoffs, and fodder.
key_patterns: whenever a creature you control dies
sacrifice.strategy_directive: SACRIFICE/ARISTOCRATS BUILD — Construct the deck around a sacrifice engine. You MUST include: (1) At least 6 free or repeatable sacrifice outlets (e.g., cards with 'sacrifice a creature' as cost or ability), (2) At least 8 death-trigger payoffs (cards that trigger 'when a creature dies' or 'whenever a creature you control dies'), (3) Token generators to supply sacrifice fodder (cards producing creature tokens), (4) Graveyard recursion to loop key pieces. Weight heavily toward low-CMC creatures with ETB or death triggers. Avoid high-CMC cards that don't contribute to the sacrifice loop.

blink.description: Abuse enters-the-battlefield triggers by repeatedly blinking or bouncing creatures.
key_patterns: return.*to the battlefield under your control
blink.strategy_directive: BLINK/ETB BUILD — Construct the deck around enters-the-battlefield abuse. You MUST include: (1) At least 6 blink/flicker enablers (spells or permanents that exile and return creatures), (2) At least 10 creatures with powerful ETB triggers (draw, removal, ramp, token production), (3) ETB doublers like Panharmonicon effects, (4) Bounce and recursion to reuse ETB creatures. Heavily prioritize creatures whose power lives in their ETB trigger. Avoid 'vanilla' creatures with no ETB value.

graveyard.description: Fill the graveyard and reanimate or recur powerful permanents for value.
graveyard.strategy_directive: GRAVEYARD/REANIMATOR BUILD — Construct the deck to exploit the graveyard as a resource. You MUST include: (1) At least 6 self-mill or discard enablers to fill the graveyard quickly (surveil, dredge, loot effects), (2) At least 6 reanimation spells or abilities (put creature from graveyard onto battlefield), (3) Graveyard payoff cards (flashback, escape, 'from your graveyard' effects), (4) Graveyard protection or recursion loops. Prioritize creatures that are worth cheating into play or that have abilities relevant from the graveyard.

tokens.description: Flood the board with creature tokens, then win with wide attacks or payoffs.
tokens.strategy_directive: TOKEN/GO-WIDE BUILD — Construct the deck around token generation and exploitation. You MUST include: (1) At least 8 reliable token generators (spells, creatures, or enchantments that create multiple creature tokens), (2) At least 5 token anthems or global buffs (cards that give all your creatures +X/+X or relevant abilities), (3) Token payoffs (cards that scale with number of creatures/tokens you control), (4) A wide-attack win condition. Prioritize token generators that produce multiple tokens per activation and avoid narrow single-target effects.

storm.description: Cast as many instants and sorceries as possible to trigger payoffs and storm off.
key_patterns: whenever you cast an instant or sorcery
storm.strategy_directive: STORM/SPELL-SLINGER BUILD — Construct the deck to chain as many spells as possible each turn. You MUST include: (1) At least 8 cantrips and cheap draw spells to chain plays, (2) At least 6 magecraft or 'whenever you cast a spell' payoffs, (3) Spell copy effects (fork, replicate, copy spell), (4) Mana rituals and fast mana to fuel spell chains, (5) A spell-based win condition (Aetherflux Reservoir pattern, storm finisher, damage dealer). Strongly prefer instants and sorceries over creatures and prioritize low-CMC spells.

voltron.description: Attach auras and equipment to one creature and deal 21 commander damage to win.
voltron.strategy_directive: VOLTRON BUILD — Construct the deck to power up one creature (usually the commander) to deal lethal commander damage. You MUST include: (1) At least 10 equipment or auras that provide +power, evasion, or protection, (2) Equipment tutors (search for equipment cards), (3) At least 3 haste enablers so the commander can attack immediately, (4) Evasion tools (flying, trample, unblockable effects), (5) Hexproof/shroud or protection effects. Prioritize low-equip-cost equipment and avoid useless combat tricks that don't permanently buff the commander.

landfall.description: Trigger landfall abilities by playing multiple lands each turn.
landfall.strategy_directive: LANDFALL BUILD — Construct the deck to trigger landfall as many times per turn as possible. You MUST include: (1) At least 8 landfall payoff cards (cards with 'whenever a land enters the battlefield under your control'), (2) At least 5 extra land drop enablers (effects that let you play additional lands per turn), (3) Land fetch and ramp spells that put lands directly onto the battlefield (not just into hand), (4) Land recursion to replay lands from the graveyard. Prioritize ramp spells that put lands into play over mana rocks. Reduce mana rocks in favor of land-based ramp.

counters.description: Place +1/+1 counters on creatures and proliferate to scale up the board.
counters.strategy_directive: COUNTERS/PROLIFERATE BUILD — Construct the deck around +1/+1 counter synergies. You MUST include: (1) At least 6 ways to place +1/+1 counters reliably (ETB counters, evolve, proliferate triggers), (2) At least 4 proliferate effects to double up counters, (3) Counter payoffs (cards that benefit from creatures having counters or that scale with counter counts), (4) A finisher that uses counters (infect alternative win, large creature swings). Prioritize creatures that enter with counters or grow each turn.

tribal.description: Fill the deck with creatures of one type and use tribal lords and synergies.
tribal.strategy_directive: TRIBAL BUILD — Construct the deck around a single creature type. You MUST include: (1) At least 8 creatures of the target tribe, (2) At least 4 tribal lords (cards giving +1/+1 or abilities to the tribe), (3) Tribal synergy cards (cards that care about creature type or reward casting creatures of a type), (4) Tribal tutors or card draw specific to the tribe. Heavily weight toward creatures of the tribal type. Avoid generic value cards that don't interact with the tribe.

enchantress.description: Play enchantments to trigger enchantress draw effects and constellation abilities.
enchantress.strategy_directive: ENCHANTRESS BUILD — Construct the deck around enchantment density for draw and constellation triggers. You MUST include: (1) At least 4 enchantress draw engines (cards that draw whenever an enchantment enters), (2) At least 6 constellation payoffs (cards with 'whenever an enchantment enters the battlefield'), (3) High enchantment density — prefer enchantments over instants/sorceries for non-land slots, (4) Aura-based protection for the commander. Replace removal instants with enchantment-based removal (Oblivion Ring effects).

artifact.description: Use artifacts and artifact synergies to generate value and win.
artifact.strategy_directive: ARTIFACT BUILD — Construct the deck to maximize artifact density and exploit artifact synergies. You MUST include: (1) At least 8 artifact permanents beyond mana rocks (preferably ones that generate value or synergize), (2) At least 5 artifact payoffs (cards that care about artifacts entering, being sacrificed, or artifact count), (3) Artifact tutors to find key pieces, (4) Mana rocks that double as artifact synergy pieces. Prioritize artifacts over non-artifact spells where functionally equal.

reanimator.description: Cheat large creatures into play via reanimation rather than paying their mana cost.
reanimator.strategy_directive: REANIMATOR BUILD — Construct the deck to cheat large creatures into play via the graveyard. You MUST include: (1) At least 5 reanimation spells (effects that put creature cards from graveyard onto battlefield), (2) At least 4 discard outlets or self-mill effects to get creatures into the graveyard quickly, (3) At least 5 high-impact reanimation targets (large creatures worth cheating into play), (4) Graveyard protection. Prioritize low-CMC enablers and discard outlets, then large splashy creatures as the payload.

mill.description: Mill opponents' libraries or self-mill to fuel graveyard synergies.
mill.strategy_directive: MILL BUILD — Construct the deck to mill opponents as the primary win condition, or self-mill to fuel graveyard plays. You MUST include: (1) At least 6 high-volume mill effects (spells or permanents that mill multiple cards), (2) Mill payoffs or win conditions (Thassa's Oracle pattern, or damage from mill), (3) Ways to protect your mill engine (counterspells, removal), (4) Draw spells to chain mill effects. Prioritize repeatable mill over one-shot effects.

lifegain.description: Gain life to trigger payoffs and drain opponents with life-based effects.
lifegain.strategy_directive: LIFEGAIN BUILD — Construct the deck around gaining life to trigger payoffs. You MUST include: (1) At least 6 reliable lifegain triggers (preferably repeatable, not one-shot), (2) At least 5 lifegain payoffs (cards that trigger 'whenever you gain life' or scale with life total), (3) A life-drain win condition (damage players equal to life gained, or turbo-large life totals), (4) Lifelink creatures and effects. Prioritize cards that gain life repeatedly each turn over large one-time life bursts.

control.description: Counter threats, wrath the board, and win through inevitability.
control.strategy_directive: CONTROL BUILD — Construct the deck to answer threats and win through card advantage. You MUST include: (1) At least 6 counterspells or permission spells, (2) At least 4 board wipes (destroy or exile all creatures), (3) At least 6 targeted removal spells, (4) At least 4 card draw engines for sustained advantage, (5) A resilient win condition that is hard to answer. Heavily prioritize interaction over proactive threats. Include draw spells and tutors to find your answers.

ramp.description: Accelerate mana production to cast large spells ahead of curve.
ramp.strategy_directive: RAMP BUILD — Construct the deck to accelerate mana and cast large payoff spells. You MUST include: (1) At least 10 ramp pieces (land-fetching spells AND mana rocks), (2) At least 5 large payoff spells or creatures that benefit from the extra mana, (3) Card draw to maintain hand size after ramping, (4) A ramp-based win condition. Weight toward land ramp over mana rocks for stability. Include both two-drop and three-drop ramp spells to avoid dead draws.
